title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston kicks off its summer festivities with the Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular on July 4, featuring performances and a drone show.
Gillette Stadium will host Boston's final World Cup quarterfinal on July 9, causing expected traffic congestion around Foxborough.
Sail Boston's Parade of Sail on July 11 showcases over 60 tall ships, beginning a five-day waterfront celebration.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant.
The restaurant's rich history includes connections to presidents, celebrities, and Revolutionary War-era events.
Union Oyster House serves around 1,500 guests daily, with clam chowder and oysters among its popular offerings.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House, Boston's historic eatery, has hosted six presidents including Kennedy and Obama.
Owner Joseph Milano commissioned a 2003 painting depicting over 50 famous guests like Al Pacino and Meryl Streep.
President Obama ordered clam chowder during his visit to Union Oyster House in 2015.

title: N.H. State Police announce unexpected death of 19-year veteran
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sgt. Brian J. Ross, a 19-year New Hampshire State Police veteran, died unexpectedly at age 41 in Greenland, NH.
Ross served in roles including the Major Crime Unit and was commander of the Collision Analysis and Reconstruction Unit.
Funeral services are scheduled for July 7 at the United Church of Christ in North Hampton; public visiting hours on July 6.

title: Jaylen Brown says Celtics showed ‘lack of respect’ after trade to Sixers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticizes Boston Celtics for lack of communication following his trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Brad Stevens facilitated Jaylen Brown's trade for Paul George, prompting criticism for lack of transparency.
Brown, now with 76ers, expressed dissatisfaction with Celtics' handling of trade and plans to earn respect in Philadelphia.

title: Hull man dies after apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach, officials say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 76-year-old Hull man, Robert Nolan, died from apparent drowning at Nantasket Beach on Wednesday evening.
Officials responded to reports of an unresponsive man in the water; Nolan was pronounced dead at 8:40 p.m.
The Plymouth County District Attorney's office stated that no foul play is suspected in the incident.

title: Don Sweeney, Bruins’ retooling plans remain a work in progress
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins moved goalie Joonas Korpisalo to the Rangers for cap relief, acquiring defenseman Will Borgen in separate deals.
Boston Bruins made multiple signings, including defenseman Connor Clifton and re-signed Jordan Harris during NHL free agency.
General Manager Don Sweeney indicated more roster adjustments are expected as the Bruins manage $5.3 million in cap space.

title: Parking will be free at state parks, beaches for Fourth of July weekend
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Free parking at state parks and beaches from July 3-5 marks the 250th anniversary of the American Revolution.
Governor Maura Healey announced the parking fee waiver at all Department of Conservation and Recreation sites.
Some North Shore beaches closed due to untreated sewage in the Merrimack River; updates available on the DCR Park Alerts webpage.

title: What’s up with those online ads for gas-saving gadgets?
author: John Paul
bullets: Online ads for gas-saving gadgets such as pills and magnets lack verified effectiveness, according to AAA Car Doctor John Paul.
Run-flat tires have improved but still offer a harsher ride and higher costs compared to conventional tires.
AAA Car Doctor John Paul advises replacing a 24-year-old Honda Accord's timing belt due to its age despite low mileage.

title: Boston civilian board recommends changes to BPD policies on bodycam footage, federal entanglements
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston's OPAT recommends releasing bodycam footage of critical incidents to the public within 30 days after family consultations.
OPAT urges Boston Police to limit collaborations with federal agencies like FBI and ICE to protect community trust and civil rights.
Recommendations include prohibiting sharing fingerprints of non-felony arrestees and enhancing oversight on Boston Regional Intelligence Center data sharing.
